blurb: The parable of the tares reveals the Father's patience with a mixed world, allowing wheat and weeds to grow together until the final harvest rather than demanding immediate purification through law. Where the Demiurge's system seeks to uproot evil through judgment and regulation, the Stranger God's wisdom recognizes that premature separation often destroys the very good it seeks to preserve. This divine restraint flows not from indifference to righteousness but from a love so profound that it risks the presence of evil rather than endanger a single child of grace.

verses.text: Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the tares of the field.
He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good seed is the Son of man;
The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the tares are the children of the wicked one;
The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
